label: Bitbucket
description: Read repositories, pull requests, and pipelines from your Bitbucket Cloud workspace.

form.title: Connect Bitbucket
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Bitbucket repositories and pull requests.

methods.label: App Password
methods.description: Paste your Bitbucket username and an app password — a scoped credential you generate in account settings. No OAuth client setup required; takes under a minute.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Bitbucket App Password

App passwords are scoped credentials tied to your Bitbucket account. They are separate from your login password and can be revoked at any time.

### Steps

1. Log in to [bitbucket.org](https://bitbucket.org) and click your **avatar** in the bottom-left corner.
2. Go to **Personal settings** (or **Account settings**).
3. In the left sidebar, click **App passwords** under the **Access management** section.
4. Click **Create app password**.
5. Give it a descriptive label, e.g. `Anton`.
6. Select the scopes Anton needs. For read access to repositories and PRs, check at minimum:
   - **Account: Read**
   - **Repositories: Read**
   - **Pull requests: Read**
   - Add **Write** scopes if you want Anton to comment or merge.
7. Click **Create**. Bitbucket displays the app password **once** — copy it immediately.
8. Paste your **Bitbucket username** (not your email) and the app password into the fields below.

### Notes

- Your username is visible at [bitbucket.org/account/settings](https://bitbucket.org/account/settings) under **Bitbucket profile settings → Username**.
- The app password is shown only once. If you lose it, delete it and create a new one.
- App passwords are per-user, not per-workspace — one credential can span multiple workspaces your account belongs to.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Bitbucket Username
fields.description: Your Bitbucket username (not your email address). Find it at Account settings → Username.

fields.name: app_password
fields.label: App Password
fields.description: Generated in Bitbucket → Personal settings → App passwords. Shown only once — copy it before closing the dialog.

fields.name: workspace
fields.label: Workspace Slug (optional)
fields.description: Limit Anton to a specific workspace slug. Leave blank to access all workspaces your account belongs to.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Authorize via Bitbucket's OAuth 2.0 flow using your own Atlassian OAuth consumer. A browser window opens for consent; no password is stored.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with OAuth 2.0

This method requires you to register an OAuth consumer in your Bitbucket workspace settings.

### Steps

1. Log in to [bitbucket.org](https://bitbucket.org) and navigate to your **Workspace settings** (the workspace you want Anton to access).
2. In the sidebar, go to **Apps and features → OAuth consumers**.
3. Click **Add consumer**.
4. Set a **Name** (e.g. `Anton`) and a **Callback URL** of `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local redirect).
5. Under **Permissions**, enable at minimum:
   - **Account: Read**
   - **Repositories: Read**
   - **Pull requests: Read**
6. Click **Save**. Bitbucket shows your **Key** (Client ID) and **Secret** (Client Secret).
7. Paste both into the fields below and click **Submit**.
8. A browser window opens to Bitbucket's consent screen — approve the access.

### Notes

- OAuth consumers live at the workspace level; they only grant access to that workspace's repositories.
- Bitbucket issues short-lived access tokens with a refresh token — Anton stores the refresh token locally to re-authenticate silently.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Consumer Key (Client ID)
fields.description: The Key shown on your OAuth consumer in Workspace settings → Apps and features → OAuth consumers.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Consumer Secret (Client Secret)
fields.description: The Secret paired with your OAuth consumer key. Treat it like a password.
